title: Install Replacement Timing Belt and Set Belt Tension
summary: Install a new timing belt, position the counter-balance carriage and idler assembly, set belt tension using equal tension screw adjustment and sonic tension meter verification, secure the idler assembly and belt clamps, reinstall side panels, and restart the operator station using the separately referenced procedure.
when_to_use: Use after timing belt replacement when performing the documented installation and tension-setting portion of the procedure for the OptiSweep timing belt.
not_for: Do not use as a standalone operator station restart procedure; the source only references 'Starting The Operator Station' on page 66 and does not provide those steps here.
Do not use when acceptable belt tension readings cannot be achieved or three acceptable readings in a row are not obtained.
Do not use if belt clamp alignment with the belt teeth cannot be achieved without pinching the belt.

responsibilities: Perform mechanical timing belt installation and tension adjustment.
Verify belt tension with the specified sonic tension meter settings when using a Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter.
Secure the idler assembly and belt clamps.
Reinstall side panels and reference the separate operator station restart procedure.

safety_notes: This procedure involves mechanical work on belt, carriage, pulley, and clamp components and is safer for L2_support.
Maintain equal adjustment of the two tension screws so they apply the same amount of force.
Excessive ambient noise such as forklifts beeping or conveyors running can interfere with sonic tension meter readings.
Ensure belt clamps align with the belt teeth and do not pinch the belt.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to counter-balance carriage and lower hard stop
Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter if that meter is used
Six M6 screws for the A-axis belt clamp
Six M6 screws for the counter-balance belt clamp
Referenced 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure on page 66

access_required: Access to the timing belt path and pulleys
system_preconditions: Timing belt removal has already been completed and the system is ready for installation.
The counter-balance carriage can be positioned to the lower hard stop and later to mid-travel.
The idler pulley assembly fasteners and tension screws are available for adjustment.
external_references: Starting The Operator Station procedure on page 66

visual_references.description: Timing belt maintenance diagram for belt location and installation context.
Idler pulley assembly maintenance figure for identifying the idler assembly during tensioning and locking.
Timing pulley maintenance diagram for pulley identification during belt placement.
Counterbalance-related maintenance diagram for identifying the counter-balance area referenced in the procedure.

steps.title: Place the new timing belt on the pulleys
steps.purpose: Install the replacement belt onto the drive path before tensioning and clamp reattachment.
steps.instruction: Place the new timing belt on the pulleys.
steps.expected_result: The replacement timing belt is seated on the pulleys.
steps.healthy_condition: The belt is installed on the pulley path and ready for carriage positioning and tensioning.
steps.failure_condition: The belt cannot be placed correctly on the pulleys or does not seat on the pulley path.

actions.label: Install timing belt
actions.exact_action: Place the new timing belt on the pulleys.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing belt location and routing context.
Pulley identification while placing the belt.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the belt cannot be placed correctly on the pulleys.

source_evidence.text: Place the new timing belt on the pulleys.

steps.title: Lower the counter-balance carriage to the lower hard stop
steps.purpose: Position the counter-balance carriage for the documented installation setup.
steps.instruction: Slightly lift the counter-balance carriage, remove the M6 fastener supporting it, and lower the counter-balance carriage to rest on the lower hard stop.
steps.expected_result: The supporting M6 fastener is removed and the counter-balance carriage rests on the lower hard stop.
steps.healthy_condition: The counter-balance carriage is resting on the lower hard stop as specified.
steps.failure_condition: The carriage cannot be safely lowered to the lower hard stop or the support fastener cannot be removed.

actions.label: Remove counter-balance support fastener
actions.exact_action: Slightly lift the counter-balance carriage, remove the M6 fastener supporting it, and lower the counter-balance carriage to rest on the lower hard stop.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counter-balance area referenced for carriage positioning.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the counter-balance carriage cannot be positioned to rest on the lower hard stop.

source_evidence.text: Slightly lift the counter-balance carriage, remove the M6 fastener supporting it, and lower the counter-balance carriage to rest on the lower hard stop.

steps.title: Loosely position the idler pulley assembly
steps.purpose: Prepare the idler pulley assembly so it can slide during tension adjustment.
steps.instruction: Very lightly tighten the M8x25 screws holding the idler pulley assembly so the assembly is not loose but can still slide under the fasteners.
steps.expected_result: The idler pulley assembly is retained but still able to slide for adjustment.
steps.healthy_condition: The assembly is not loose and still slides under the fasteners.
steps.failure_condition: The assembly is either too loose to hold position or too tight to slide for adjustment.

actions.label: Set idler assembly to sliding hold
actions.exact_action: Very lightly tighten the M8x25 screws holding the idler pulley assembly so the assembly is not loose but can still slide under the fasteners.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly being held by the M8x25 fasteners.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the idler pulley assembly cannot be set so it is retained but still slides.

source_evidence.text: Very lightly tighten the M8x25 screws holding the idler pulley assembly so the assembly is not loose but can still slide under the fasteners.

steps.title: Seat the tension screws against the actuator base
steps.purpose: Prepare the tensioning mechanism for controlled belt tension adjustment.
steps.instruction: Insert the two M6x35 tension screws until they make firm contact with the actuator base.
steps.expected_result: Both tension screws are installed and in firm contact with the actuator base.
steps.healthy_condition: Each M6x35 tension screw is seated firmly against the actuator base.
steps.failure_condition: One or both tension screws cannot be brought into firm contact with the actuator base.

actions.label: Install tension screws
actions.exact_action: Insert the two M6x35 tension screws until they make firm contact with the actuator base.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the tension screws cannot be seated firmly against the actuator base.

source_evidence.text: Insert the two M6x35 tension screws until they make firm contact with the actuator base.

steps.title: Apply belt tension evenly with both tension screws
steps.purpose: Increase belt tension while keeping force balanced across the idler adjustment.
steps.instruction: Equally tighten the two tension screws to add belt tension, making sure both screws are turned the exact same amount so they apply the same amount of force.
steps.expected_result: Belt tension increases while both screws remain equally adjusted.
steps.healthy_condition: Both tension screws are turned the exact same amount and apply the same amount of force.
steps.failure_condition: Equal adjustment of the two tension screws cannot be maintained.

actions.label: Equalize tension screw adjustment
actions.exact_action: Equally tighten the two tension screws to add belt tension, making sure both screws are turned the exact same amount so they apply the same amount of force.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler assembly adjustment area where equal tension screw movement is applied.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if equal adjustment of the two tension screws cannot be maintained, because both screws need to apply the same amount of force.

source_evidence.text: Equally tighten the two tension screws to add belt tension, making sure both screws are turned the exact same amount so they apply the same amount of force.
Stop if equal adjustment of the two tension screws cannot be maintained, because the source states both screws need to apply the same amount of force.

steps.title: Measure belt tension on the counter-balance leg only
steps.purpose: Ensure the tension check is performed at the correct belt location.
steps.instruction: Check belt tension only on the counter-balance leg of the belt.
steps.expected_result: The tension measurement is taken from the specified belt leg.
steps.healthy_condition: The sonic tension check is performed only on the counter-balance leg of the belt.
steps.failure_condition: Tension is checked on the wrong belt segment.

actions.label: Check correct belt leg
actions.exact_action: Check belt tension only on the counter-balance leg of the belt.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing belt path to identify the counter-balance leg for measurement.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the correct counter-balance leg cannot be identified for the tension check.

source_evidence.text: Check belt tension only on the counter-balance leg of the belt.

steps.title: Set the Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter parameters
steps.purpose: Configure the specified sonic tension meter with the documented measurement parameters.
steps.instruction: If using a Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter, set Mass to 4.86 g/m, Width to 20 mm, and Span to 1200 mm.
steps.expected_result: The Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter is configured with the documented values.
steps.healthy_condition: Mass is 4.86 g/m, Width is 20 mm, and Span is 1200 mm on the Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter.
steps.failure_condition: The meter cannot be configured to the documented values or a different unsupported configuration is used.

actions.label: Configure sonic tension meter
actions.exact_action: If using a Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter, set Mass to 4.86 g/m, Width to 20 mm, and Span to 1200 mm.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter cannot be set to Mass 4.86 g/m, Width 20 mm, and Span 1200 mm when that meter is being used.

source_evidence.text: If using a Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter, set Mass to 4.86 g/m, Width to 20 mm, and Span to 1200 mm.

steps.title: Adjust tension until three acceptable readings in a row are obtained
steps.purpose: Bring the belt into the documented acceptable tension range and verify repeatable readings.
steps.instruction: Gradually increase and adjust the belt tension until 471 N is reached, with an acceptable range between 448 N and 494 N, and continue adjusting/checking until three acceptable readings in a row are received.
steps.expected_result: The belt tension is verified at 471 N target, within the acceptable range of 448 N to 494 N, with three acceptable readings in a row.
steps.healthy_condition: Three consecutive acceptable readings are obtained within the 448 N to 494 N range, targeting 471 N.
steps.failure_condition: Acceptable belt tension readings cannot be obtained or three acceptable readings in a row are not achieved.

actions.label: Verify belt tension range
actions.exact_action: Gradually increase and adjust the belt tension until 471 N is reached, with an acceptable range between 448 N and 494 N, and continue adjusting/checking until three acceptable readings in a row are received.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if acceptable belt tension readings cannot be obtained.
Stop if three acceptable readings in a row are not achieved.

source_evidence.text: Gradually increase and adjust the belt tension until 471 N is reached, with an acceptable range between 448 N and 494 N, and continue adjusting/checking until three acceptable readings in a row are received.
Stop if acceptable belt tension readings cannot be obtained or if three acceptable readings in a row are not achieved.

steps.title: Control ambient noise during sonic tension measurement
steps.purpose: Reduce measurement interference that can affect sonic meter readings.
steps.instruction: Account for excessive area noise because forklifts beeping, conveyors running, or similar noise can interfere with sonic meter readings.
steps.expected_result: Sonic tension readings are taken with awareness of ambient noise interference.
steps.healthy_condition: Readings are obtained without obvious interference from excessive area noise.
steps.failure_condition: Ambient noise interferes with sonic meter readings.

actions.label: Assess ambient noise
actions.exact_action: Account for excessive area noise because forklifts beeping, conveyors running, or similar noise can interfere with sonic meter readings.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or delay measurement if excessive ambient noise is interfering with sonic meter readings.

source_evidence.text: Account for excessive area noise because forklifts beeping, conveyors running, or similar noise can interfere with sonic meter readings.
Be aware that excessive ambient noise can interfere with sonic tension meter readings.

steps.title: Lock the idler assembly in place
steps.purpose: Secure the idler pulley assembly after final belt tension is verified.
steps.instruction: Tighten the M8x25 fasteners to 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs) to lock the idler assembly in place.
steps.expected_result: The idler assembly is locked in place at the specified torque.
steps.healthy_condition: The M8x25 fasteners are tightened to 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs) and the idler assembly is secured.
steps.failure_condition: The fasteners cannot be tightened to the specified torque or the idler assembly does not lock in place.

actions.label: Torque idler fasteners
actions.exact_action: Tighten the M8x25 fasteners to 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs) to lock the idler assembly in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly fastener location for final locking.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the M8x25 fasteners cannot be tightened to 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs).

source_evidence.text: Tighten the M8x25 fasteners to 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs) to lock the idler assembly in place.

steps.title: Raise the counter-balance carriage to mid-travel and reinstall support screws
steps.purpose: Reposition the counter-balance carriage and restore the documented support screws.
steps.instruction: Raise the counter-balance carriage back to mid-travel and insert the M6 socket-head screws for the carriage to rest against.
steps.expected_result: The counter-balance carriage is at mid-travel and the M6 socket-head screws are inserted.
steps.healthy_condition: The carriage is supported at mid-travel by the inserted M6 socket-head screws.
steps.failure_condition: The carriage cannot be raised to mid-travel or the M6 socket-head screws cannot be inserted.

actions.label: Reposition counter-balance carriage
actions.exact_action: Raise the counter-balance carriage back to mid-travel and insert the M6 socket-head screws for the carriage to rest against.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counter-balance area while returning the carriage to mid-travel.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the counter-balance carriage cannot be raised to mid-travel or supported as described.

source_evidence.text: Raise the counter-balance carriage back to mid-travel and insert the M6 socket-head screws for the carriage to rest against.

steps.title: Re-attach the A-axis belt clamp
steps.purpose: Secure the A-axis side of the belt without damaging or misaligning the belt.
steps.instruction: Re-attach the A-axis belt clamp using the six M6 screws and Loctite®, making sure the clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt; move the belt slightly if needed so the clamp lines up properly.
steps.expected_result: The A-axis belt clamp is reattached with six M6 screws and Loctite®, aligned to the belt teeth without pinching the belt.
steps.healthy_condition: The clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt.
steps.failure_condition: The clamp cannot be aligned with the belt teeth or pinches the belt.

actions.label: Attach A-axis belt clamp
actions.exact_action: Re-attach the A-axis belt clamp using the six M6 screws and Loctite®, making sure the clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt; move the belt slightly if needed so the clamp lines up properly.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing belt tooth alignment context while reattaching the A-axis clamp.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the belt clamp cannot be aligned with the belt teeth without pinching the belt.

source_evidence.text: Re-attach the A-axis belt clamp using the six M6 screws and Loctite®, making sure the clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt; move the belt slightly if needed so the clamp lines up properly.
Escalate if the belt clamps cannot be aligned with the belt teeth without pinching the belt.

steps.title: Re-attach the counter-balance belt clamp
steps.purpose: Secure the counter-balance side of the belt without damaging or misaligning the belt.
steps.instruction: Re-attach the counter-balance belt clamp using the six M6 screws and Loctite®, making sure the clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt; lift the counter-balance slightly if needed so the clamp lines up properly.
steps.expected_result: The counter-balance belt clamp is reattached with six M6 screws and Loctite®, aligned to the belt teeth without pinching the belt.
steps.healthy_condition: The clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt.
steps.failure_condition: The clamp cannot be aligned with the belt teeth or pinches the belt.

actions.label: Attach counter-balance belt clamp
actions.exact_action: Re-attach the counter-balance belt clamp using the six M6 screws and Loctite®, making sure the clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt; lift the counter-balance slightly if needed so the clamp lines up properly.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing belt tooth alignment context while reattaching the counter-balance clamp.
Counter-balance area referenced when slight lifting is needed for clamp alignment.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the belt clamp cannot be aligned with the belt teeth without pinching the belt.

source_evidence.text: Re-attach the counter-balance belt clamp using the six M6 screws and Loctite®, making sure the clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt; lift the counter-balance slightly if needed so the clamp lines up properly.
Escalate if the belt clamps cannot be aligned with the belt teeth without pinching the belt.

steps.title: Remove the temporary support screws
steps.purpose: Remove the temporary M6 socket-head screws used to support the A-axis and counter-balance.
steps.instruction: Remove the M6 socket-head screws supporting the A-axis and counter-balance.
steps.expected_result: The temporary support screws are removed from the A-axis and counter-balance supports.
steps.healthy_condition: No temporary M6 socket-head support screws remain installed.
steps.failure_condition: The support screws cannot be removed or the A-axis/counter-balance cannot remain properly supported afterward.

actions.label: Remove support screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the M6 socket-head screws supporting the A-axis and counter-balance.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the temporary support screws cannot be removed safely.

source_evidence.text: Remove the M6 socket-head screws supporting the A-axis and counter-balance.

steps.title: Reinstall the side panels
steps.purpose: Restore the machine enclosure after the belt installation work is complete.
steps.healthy_condition: The side panels are back in place after maintenance.
steps.failure_condition: The side panels cannot be reinstalled correctly.
stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the side panels cannot be reinstalled correctly.

source_evidence.text: Reinstall the side panels.

steps.title: Restart the operator station using the referenced procedure
steps.purpose: Return the operator station to service using the separately documented startup procedure.
steps.instruction: Re-start the operator station using the referenced 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure on page 66.
steps.expected_result: The operator station restart is handed off to the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station is restarted using the referenced page 66 procedure.
steps.failure_condition: The operator station cannot be restarted or the referenced procedure is unavailable.

actions.label: Reference operator station startup
actions.exact_action: Re-start the operator station using the referenced 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure on page 66.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the referenced 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure on page 66 is unavailable or restart cannot be completed.

source_evidence.text: Re-start the operator station using the referenced 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure on page 66.
The source references restarting the operator station on page 66 but does not include those restart steps here, so they are not expanded.

success_criteria: The replacement timing belt is installed on the pulleys.
Belt tension is verified on the counter-balance leg using the documented method.
If using a Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter, the meter is set to Mass 4.86 g/m, Width 20 mm, and Span 1200 mm.
Belt tension reaches the 471 N target within the acceptable range of 448 N to 494 N.
Three acceptable belt tension readings in a row are obtained.
The M8x25 fasteners are tightened to 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs) to lock the idler assembly in place.
Both belt clamps are reattached with six M6 screws and Loctite®, aligned with the belt teeth, without pinching the belt.
The operator station restart is performed using the referenced page 66 procedure.
healthy_conditions: The timing belt is seated on the pulleys.
The counter-balance carriage rests on the lower hard stop when required and is later returned to mid-travel.
The idler pulley assembly is initially able to slide under the fasteners and is later locked in place.
Both tension screws are adjusted equally and apply the same amount of force.
Tension readings are taken only on the counter-balance leg of the belt.
Three consecutive acceptable readings are obtained within 448 N to 494 N.
Belt clamps align with the belt teeth and do not pinch the belt.
failure_conditions: Equal adjustment of the two tension screws cannot be maintained.
Acceptable belt tension readings cannot be obtained.
Three acceptable readings in a row are not achieved.
Excessive ambient noise interferes with sonic meter readings.
The belt clamps cannot be aligned with the belt teeth without pinching the belt.
The idler assembly cannot be locked in place at 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs).
The side panels cannot be reinstalled correctly.
The referenced operator station startup procedure cannot be completed.
escalation_guidance: Stop if equal adjustment of the two tension screws cannot be maintained.
Stop if acceptable belt tension readings cannot be obtained or if three acceptable readings in a row are not achieved.
Delay or repeat measurement if excessive ambient noise is interfering with sonic meter readings.
Escalate if the belt clamps cannot be aligned with the belt teeth without pinching the belt.
Escalate if the referenced 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure on page 66 is unavailable or restart cannot be completed.
